item.title: Credential proxy architecture — host-side token proxy so secrets never enter the container
item.intent: Storing API tokens inside the agent container is a single-exploit-away from credential theft. A host-side credential proxy means the container never holds tokens.
item.description: Clawdius recon (2026-03-29): cred-proxy on Docker bridge holds all API tokens on host, injects auth headers. ClawHQ has src/secure/credentials/ but no proxy architecture.
steps: Design credential proxy service: Docker sidecar, route definitions, header injection
Generate cred-proxy docker-compose service during clawhq init
Update tool generators to call cred-proxy URL
Add audit logging for proxied requests
Add doctor check: cred-proxy reachable, routes healthy
acceptanceCriteria: Credential proxy runs as host-side sidecar
API tokens never in agent container
